title: bupleurum graminifolium var. lonchophorum
intro: Bupleurum graminifolium var. lonchophorum, also known as Chinese chaff flower, is a plant species that belongs to the family Apiaceae, characterized by its small yellow flowers and narrow, grass-like foliage. It has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiviral effects, which are attributed to its bioactive compounds such as saikosaponins and flavonoids. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections, and promote liver function. Bupleurum graminifolium var. lonchophorum is often used in herbal preparations, such as decoctions and infusions, to support immune function and overall well-being.
